import pytest
from API_tests.conftest import register_and_login


def _credit_user(client, admin_headers, uid, amount=500.0):
    """Give a user credits so they pass the guard threshold."""
    client.post('/api/ledger/credit', headers=admin_headers,
                json={'user_id': uid, 'amount': amount, 'description': 'Test top-up'})


class TestAdminUsers:
    def test_list_users_admin(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/admin/users', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert 'users' in data

    def test_list_users_non_admin_forbidden(self, client, user_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/admin/users', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_user_detail(self, client, admin_headers, user_headers_with_id):
        _, uid = user_headers_with_id
        resp = client.get(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert 'user' in data
        assert '***' in data['user']['email']


class TestViolations:
    def test_report_violation(self, client, admin_headers,
                               user_headers_with_id, user2_headers_with_id):
        headers1, uid1 = user_headers_with_id
        _, uid2 = user2_headers_with_id

        resp = client.post('/api/admin/violations', headers=headers1,
                           json={
                               'target_id': uid2,
                               'violation_type': 'spam',
                               'description': 'Sending spam messages',
                               'severity': 'low'
                           })
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        assert 'violation_id' in resp.get_json()

    def test_list_violations_admin(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/admin/violations', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert 'violations' in resp.get_json()

    def test_cannot_report_self(self, client, user_headers_with_id):
        headers, uid = user_headers_with_id
        resp = client.post('/api/admin/violations', headers=headers,
                           json={
                               'target_id': uid,
                               'violation_type': 'spam',
                               'description': 'Self-report',
                               'severity': 'low'
                           })
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_resolve_violation(self, client, admin_headers,
                                user_headers_with_id, user2_headers_with_id):
        headers1, uid1 = user_headers_with_id
        _, uid2 = user2_headers_with_id

        create = client.post('/api/admin/violations', headers=headers1,
                             json={
                                 'target_id': uid2,
                                 'violation_type': 'fraud',
                                 'description': 'Fraudulent behaviour',
                                 'severity': 'high'
                             })
        vid = create.get_json()['violation_id']

        resp = client.put(f'/api/admin/violations/{vid}',
                          headers=admin_headers,
                          json={'decision': 'resolved',
                                'notes': 'Confirmed fraud'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_dismiss_violation(self, client, admin_headers,
                                user_headers_with_id, user2_headers_with_id):
        headers1, uid1 = user_headers_with_id
        _, uid2 = user2_headers_with_id

        create = client.post('/api/admin/violations', headers=headers1,
                             json={
                                 'target_id': uid2,
                                 'violation_type': 'other',
                                 'description': 'Minor issue',
                                 'severity': 'low'
                             })
        vid = create.get_json()['violation_id']
        resp = client.put(f'/api/admin/violations/{vid}',
                          headers=admin_headers,
                          json={'decision': 'dismissed',
                                'notes': 'Not substantiated'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200


class TestBanUnban:
    def test_admin_can_ban_user(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'ban_me', 'ban_me@test.com')
        resp = client.put(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}/ban', headers=admin_headers,
                          json={'reason': 'Repeated violations'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200

        # Verify user is inactive
        detail = client.get(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}', headers=admin_headers)
        assert detail.get_json()['user']['is_active'] is False

    def test_banned_user_cannot_login(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'ban_login', 'ban_login@test.com')
        client.put(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}/ban', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'reason': 'Test ban'})

        resp = client.post('/api/auth/login', json={
            'username': 'ban_login',
            'password': 'TestUser@123456!'
        })
        assert resp.status_code in (401, 429, 403)

    def test_admin_can_unban_user(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'unban_me', 'unban_me@test.com')
        client.put(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}/ban', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'reason': 'Temp ban'})

        resp = client.put(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}/unban', headers=admin_headers,
                          json={'reason': 'Appeal upheld'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200

        detail = client.get(f'/api/admin/users/{uid}', headers=admin_headers)
        assert detail.get_json()['user']['is_active'] is True

    def test_non_admin_cannot_ban(self, client, user_headers,
                                   user2_headers_with_id):
        _, uid2 = user2_headers_with_id
        resp = client.put(f'/api/admin/users/{uid2}/ban', headers=user_headers,
                          json={'reason': 'Unauthorized'})
        assert resp.status_code == 403


class TestSessions:
    def test_list_sessions_admin(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/admin/sessions', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert 'sessions' in resp.get_json()

    def test_list_sessions_non_admin_forbidden(self, client, user_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/admin/sessions', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403


class TestAdminPermissions:
    def test_grant_permission(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'perm_admin',
                                     'perm_admin@test.com')
        # Promote to admin first
        client.put(f'/api/users/{uid}/role', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'role': 'admin'})

        resp = client.put(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/users',
                          headers=admin_headers,
                          json={'can_read': True, 'can_write': False})
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_get_permissions(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'perm_check',
                                     'perm_check@test.com')
        client.put(f'/api/users/{uid}/role', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'role': 'admin'})

        client.put(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/violations',
                   headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'can_read': True, 'can_write': True})

        resp = client.get(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_revoke_permission(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'perm_revoke',
                                     'perm_revoke@test.com')
        client.put(f'/api/users/{uid}/role', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'role': 'admin'})

        client.put(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/ledger',
                   headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'can_read': True, 'can_write': False})

        resp = client.delete(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/ledger',
                             headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
"}

        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_get_permissions(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'perm_check',
                                     'perm_check@test.com')
        client.put(f'/api/users/{uid}/role', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'role': 'admin'})

        client.put(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/violations',
                   headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'can_read': True, 'can_write': True})

        resp = client.get(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_revoke_permission(self, client, admin_headers):
        h, uid = register_and_login(client, 'perm_revoke',
                                     'perm_revoke@test.com')
        client.put(f'/api/users/{uid}/role', headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'role': 'admin'})

        client.put(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/ledger',
                   headers=admin_headers,
                   json={'can_read': True, 'can_write': False})

        resp = client.delete(f'/api/admin/permissions/{uid}/ledger',
                             headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
"}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\user\Desktop\eaglepoint_new\Week-2\TASK-57\repo\API_tests\test_analytics_api.py", "content": """"API tests for /api/analytics: KPIs, CSV export, reports."""

import pytest


class TestKPIs:
    def test_kpis_admin(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert 'kpis' in data

    def test_kpis_with_date_range(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis?from_date=2020-01-01&to_date=2030-12-31',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_kpis_invalid_date_format(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis?from_date=not-a-date',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_kpis_from_after_to_rejected(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis?from_date=2025-12-31&to_date=2025-01-01',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_kpis_non_admin_forbidden(self, client, user_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_kpis_contains_conversion_rate(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis', headers=admin_headers)
        kpis = resp.get_json()['kpis']
        assert 'conversion_rate' in kpis

    def test_kpis_contains_dispute_rate(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/kpis', headers=admin_headers)
        kpis = resp.get_json()['kpis']
        assert 'dispute_rate' in kpis


class TestCSVExport:
    def test_kpi_export(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export?type=kpi',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert resp.content_type.startswith('text/csv')
        assert len(resp.data) > 0

    def test_daily_export(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export?type=daily',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert resp.content_type.startswith('text/csv')

    def test_export_with_date_range(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export?type=kpi&from_date=2020-01-01&to_date=2030-12-31',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_invalid_export_type(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export?type=invalid',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 400
        assert 'error' in resp.get_json()

    def test_export_non_admin_forbidden(self, client, user_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_kpi_csv_has_header_row(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export?type=kpi',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        content = resp.data.decode('utf-8')
        lines = [l for l in content.splitlines() if l.strip()]
        assert len(lines) >= 1

    def test_content_disposition_header(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/export?type=kpi',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert 'Content-Disposition' in resp.headers
        assert 'attachment' in resp.headers['Content-Disposition']
        assert '.csv' in resp.headers['Content-Disposition']


class TestReports:
    def test_list_reports(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/reports', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert 'reports' in data
        assert 'total' in data

    def test_list_reports_non_admin_forbidden(self, client, user_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/reports', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_generate_report(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.post('/api/analytics/reports/generate',
                           headers=admin_headers,
                           json={})
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert 'message' in data

    def test_generate_report_with_date(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.post('/api/analytics/reports/generate',
                           headers=admin_headers,
                           json={'date': '2024-06-15'})
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_generate_report_invalid_date(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.post('/api/analytics/reports/generate',
                           headers=admin_headers,
                           json={'date': 'bad-date'})
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_get_report_latest_after_generate(self, client, admin_headers):
        # Ensure at least one report exists
        client.post('/api/analytics/reports/generate', headers=admin_headers)
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/reports/latest', headers=admin_headers)
        # Either 200 (report found) or 404 (no reports yet in edge case)
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 404)

    def test_get_nonexistent_report(self, client, admin_headers):
        resp = client.get('/api/analytics/reports/9999-99-99',
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 404

    def test_generate_report_non_admin_forbidden(self, client, user_headers):
        resp = client.post('/api/analytics/reports/generate',
                           headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403
"}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\user\Desktop\eaglepoint_new\Week-2\TASK-57\repo\README.md", "content": "# SkillShare Platform

A peer skill-exchange platform with ledger, identity verification, admin moderation, analytics, and offline payment support.

---

## Quick Start

```bash
docker compose up
```

That's it. No manual configuration or SQL imports required. The application auto-seeds an admin account and initialises the SQLite database on first run.

---

## Services

| Service | Address | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Application (API + UI) | http://localhost:8000 | Flask app — REST API and frontend |

All REST endpoints are prefixed with `/api/`. The frontend SPA is served at `/`.

---

## Default Admin Credentials

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Username | `admin` |
| Password | `Admin@123456!` |

**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**

---

## API Endpoint Summary

| Prefix | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `POST /api/auth/register` | Register a new user |
| `POST /api/auth/login` | Login and receive JWT |
| `GET  /api/auth/me` | Get current user profile |
| `GET  /api/users` | List users (admin only) |
| `GET  /api/ledger/balance` | Check credit balance |
| `POST /api/ledger/credit` | Credit a user (admin only) |
| `POST /api/ledger/transfer` | Peer credit transfer |
| `POST /api/ledger/invoices` | Create an invoice |
| `POST /api/payments/submit` | Submit offline payment (cash/check/ACH) |
| `POST /api/payments/<id>/confirm` | Confirm payment and credit account (admin) |
| `POST /api/payments/<id>/refund` | Refund a confirmed payment (admin) |
| `GET  /api/audit/logs` | Audit log listing (admin/auditor) |
| `GET  /api/audit/logs/summary` | Event counts by category |
| `GET  /api/audit/logs/verify` | SHA-256 chain integrity check |
| `GET  /api/analytics/kpis` | KPI dashboard |
| `GET  /api/analytics/export` | CSV export (kpi or daily) |
| `POST /api/admin/violations` | Report a violation |
| `PUT  /api/admin/users/<id>/ban` | Ban a user |
| `PUT  /api/admin/users/<id>/unban` | Unban a user |

---

## Step-by-Step Verification Guide

### 1. Start the application

```bash
docker compose up
```

Wait for the line: `Running on http://0.0.0.0:8000`

### 2. Verify the API is reachable

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"Admin@123456!"}' | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: `{"token": "...", "user": {"role": "admin", ...}}`

### 3. Register a user

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"alice","email":"alice@example.com","password":"Alice@123456!"}' \
  | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: `{"message": "Registration successful.", "user_id": 2}`

### 4. Credit a user (admin)

```bash
# Get admin token first
TOKEN=$(curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"Admin@123456!"}' \
  | python -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/ledger/credit \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"user_id":2,"amount":500,"description":"Welcome bonus"}' \
  | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: `{"message": "Credits added.", "new_balance": 500.0}`

### 5. Submit an offline payment

```bash
USER_TOKEN=$(curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"alice","password":"Alice@123456!"}' \
  | python -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/payments/submit \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"amount":200,"payment_type":"check","reference_number":"CHK-00001"}' \
  | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: `{"payment_id": 1, "signature": "<64-char hex>"}`

### 6. Confirm the payment (admin)

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/payments/1/confirm \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -X POST | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: `{"message": "Payment confirmed and account credited.", "new_balance": 700.0}`

### 7. Verify the audit chain

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/audit/logs/verify \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: `{"valid": true, "message": "Audit log chain is intact.", "entries": <n>}`

### 8. View the analytics dashboard

```bash
curl -s "http://localhost:8000/api/analytics/kpis" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python -m json.tool
```

Expected: JSON object with `kpis.conversion_rate`, `kpis.dispute_rate`, etc.

---

## Running Tests

```bash
bash run_tests.sh
```

The script installs dependencies if needed, then runs:
- **Unit tests** in `unit_tests/` — service logic, hash chaining, HMAC verification, state transitions, boundary conditions
- **API tests** in `API_tests/` — every REST endpoint, RBAC enforcement, error responses

Tests are idempotent and use an isolated in-memory / temp database. No manual setup required.

---

## Project Structure

```
repo/
├── app/
│   ├── dal/            # Data Access Layer (SQL only, no business logic)
│   ├── routes/         # Flask blueprints (HTTP only, no SQL)
│   ├── services/       # Business logic layer
│   ├── models.py       # Schema, migrations, db() context manager
│   └── utils.py        # Crypto, hashing, JWT, decorators
├── unit_tests/         # Pytest unit tests (service + DAL layer)
├── API_tests/          # Pytest API tests (Flask test client)
├── static/             # Frontend assets (CSS, JS)
├── templates/          # SPA entry point (index.html)
├── config.py           # Auto-generated secrets + runtime config
├── run.py              # Application entry point
├── requirements.txt    # Python dependencies
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
└── run_tests.sh        # One-command test runner
```

---

## Security Notes

- All passwords hashed with PBKDF2-SHA256 (600,000 iterations)
- JWTs signed with HS256, expire after 24 hours
- Identity documents encrypted with AES-256-GCM
- Offline payment records signed with HMAC-SHA256
- Audit log entries form a tamper-evident SHA-256 hash chain
- Ledger entries form a tamper-evident SHA-256 hash chain
- No external API calls — fully offline compliant
"}


The script installs dependencies if needed, then runs:
- **Unit tests** in `unit_tests/` — service logic, hash chaining, HMAC verification, state transitions, boundary conditions
- **API tests** in `API_tests/` — every REST endpoint, RBAC enforcement, error responses

Tests are idempotent and use an isolated in-memory / temp database. No manual setup required.

---

## Project Structure

```
repo/
├── app/
│   ├── dal/            # Data Access Layer (SQL only, no business logic)
│   ├── routes/         # Flask blueprints (HTTP only, no SQL)
│   ├── services/       # Business logic layer
│   ├── models.py       # Schema, migrations, db() context manager
│   └── utils.py        # Crypto, hashing, JWT, decorators
├── unit_tests/         # Pytest unit tests (service + DAL layer)
├── API_tests/          # Pytest API tests (Flask test client)
├── static/             # Frontend assets (CSS, JS)
├── templates/          # SPA entry point (index.html)
├── config.py           # Auto-generated secrets + runtime config
├── run.py              # Application entry point
├── requirements.txt    # Python dependencies
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
└── run_tests.sh        # One-command test runner
```

---

## Security Notes

- All passwords hashed with PBKDF2-SHA256 (600,000 iterations)
- JWTs signed with HS256, expire after 24 hours
- Identity documents encrypted with AES-256-GCM
- Offline payment records signed with HMAC-SHA256
- Audit log entries form a tamper-evident SHA-256 hash chain
- Ledger entries form a tamper-evident SHA-256 hash chain
- No external API calls — fully offline compliant
"}

                            amount=100.0):
        resp = client.post('/api/payments/submit', headers=user_headers,
                           json={'amount': amount, 'payment_type': 'cash',
                                 'reference_number': ref})
        pid = resp.get_json()['payment_id']
        client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/confirm', headers=admin_headers)
        return pid

    def test_admin_refunds_payment(self, client, admin_headers,
                                    user_headers, user_headers_with_id):
        _, uid = user_headers_with_id
        pid = self._confirmed_payment(client, admin_headers, user_headers, 'REF-001')

        bal_before = client.get(f'/api/ledger/balance?user_id={uid}',
                                 headers=admin_headers).get_json()['balance']
        resp = client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=admin_headers,
                           json={'reason': 'Duplicate charge'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert data['new_balance'] == bal_before - 100.0

    def test_refund_updates_status(self, client, admin_headers, user_headers):
        pid = self._confirmed_payment(client, admin_headers, user_headers, 'REF-002')
        client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=admin_headers)
        resp = client.get(f'/api/payments/{pid}', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.get_json()['payment']['status'] == 'refunded'

    def test_refund_pending_payment_rejected(self, client, admin_headers,
                                              user_headers):
        resp = client.post('/api/payments/submit', headers=user_headers,
                           json={'amount': 50.0, 'payment_type': 'ach',
                                 'reference_number': 'REF-PEND'})
        pid = resp.get_json()['payment_id']
        resp = client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_non_admin_refund_forbidden(self, client, admin_headers,
                                         user_headers):
        pid = self._confirmed_payment(client, admin_headers, user_headers, 'REF-003')
        resp = client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403", "new_string": "    def _confirmed_payment(self, client, admin_headers, headers, uid, ref,
                            amount=100.0):
        """Submit and confirm a payment for the user identified by (headers, uid)."""
        resp = client.post('/api/payments/submit', headers=headers,
                           json={'amount': amount, 'payment_type': 'cash',
                                 'reference_number': ref})
        pid = resp.get_json()['payment_id']
        client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/confirm', headers=admin_headers)
        return pid

    def test_admin_refunds_payment(self, client, admin_headers,
                                    user_headers_with_id):
        headers, uid = user_headers_with_id
        pid = self._confirmed_payment(client, admin_headers, headers, uid, 'REF-001')

        bal_before = client.get(f'/api/ledger/balance?user_id={uid}',
                                 headers=admin_headers).get_json()['balance']
        resp = client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=admin_headers,
                           json={'reason': 'Duplicate charge'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert data['new_balance'] == bal_before - 100.0

    def test_refund_updates_status(self, client, admin_headers,
                                    user_headers_with_id):
        headers, uid = user_headers_with_id
        pid = self._confirmed_payment(client, admin_headers, headers, uid, 'REF-002')
        client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=admin_headers)
        resp = client.get(f'/api/payments/{pid}', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.get_json()['payment']['status'] == 'refunded'

    def test_refund_pending_payment_rejected(self, client, admin_headers,
                                              user_headers):
        resp = client.post('/api/payments/submit', headers=user_headers,
                           json={'amount': 50.0, 'payment_type': 'ach',
                                 'reference_number': 'REF-PEND'})
        pid = resp.get_json()['payment_id']
        resp = client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_non_admin_refund_forbidden(self, client, admin_headers,
                                         user_headers, user_headers_with_id):
        headers2, uid2 = user_headers_with_id
        pid = self._confirmed_payment(client, admin_headers, headers2, uid2, 'REF-003')
        resp = client.post(f'/api/payments/{pid}/refund', headers=user_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 403"}

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Canonical payload signing: `{user_id, amount, payment_type, reference_number, created_at}` sorted-key JSON

31	    if payment_type not in ('cash', 'check', 'ach'):
32	        raise ValueError("payment_type must be 'cash', 'check', or 'ach'.")
33	    if not reference_number or not reference_number.strip():
34	        raise ValueError('reference_number is required.')
35	
36	    user = user_dal.get_by_id(conn, user_id)
37	    if not user:
38	        raise LookupError('User not found.')
39	
40	    # Build canonical payload — same fields reconstructed on confirm
41	    now = utcnow()
42	    canonical = {
43	        'user_id':          user_id,
44	        'amount':           amount,
45	        'payment_type':     payment_type,
46	        'reference_number': reference_number.strip(),
47	        'created_at':       now,
48	    }
49	    signature = sign_payment_payload(canonical)
50	
51	    payment_id = payment_dal.create(
52	        conn, user_id, amount, payment_type,
53	        reference_number.strip(), signature, notes
54	    )
55	    # Overwrite created_at with the exact value used for signing
56	    conn.execute(
57	        'UPDATE offline_payments SET created_at = ? WHERE id = ?',
58	        (now, payment_id)
59	    )
60	
61	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'PAYMENT_SUBMITTED', user_id=user_id,
62	                    entity_type='offline_payment', entity_id=payment_id,
63	                    details={'amount': amount, 'payment_type': payment_type,
64	                             'reference_number': reference_number.strip()})
65	    return {'payment_id': payment_id, 'signature': signature}
66	
67	
68	def confirm_payment(conn, payment_id: int, admin_id: int) -> dict:
69	    """
70	    Admin confirms a pending payment.
71	    Verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature; on failure marks the record 'failed'.
72	    On success credits the user's ledger balance and fires callback simulation.
73	    Returns {'payment_id': int, 'new_balance': float}.
74	    Raises LookupError, ValueError, or PermissionError.
75	    """
76	    payment = payment_dal.get_by_id(conn, payment_id)
77	    if not payment:
78	        raise LookupError('Payment not found.')
79	    if payment['status'] != 'pending':
80	        raise ValueError(f'Payment is already {payment["status"]}.')
81	
82	    # Re-construct the canonical payload exactly as it was at submit time
83	    canonical = {
84	        'user_id':          payment['user_id'],
85	        'amount':           payment['amount'],
86	        'payment_type':     payment['payment_type'],
87	        'reference_number': payment['reference_number'],
88	        'created_at':       payment['created_at'],
89	    }
90	    if not verify_payment_signature(canonical, payment['signature']):
91	        payment_dal.set_failed(conn, payment_id)
92	        audit_dal.write(conn, 'PAYMENT_FAILED', user_id=admin_id,
93	                        entity_type='offline_payment', entity_id=payment_id,
94	                        details={'reason': 'signature_mismatch'})
95	        raise ValueError('Payment signature verification failed.')
96	
97	    # Credit the payer's balance
98	    user = user_dal.get_by_id(conn, payment['user_id'])
99	    if not user:
100	        raise LookupError('Payer user not found.')
101	
102	    new_balance = round(user['credit_balance'] + payment['amount'], 4)
103	    user_dal.update_fields(conn, payment['user_id'], credit_balance=new_balance)
104	    ledger_dal.insert_entry(
105	        conn,
106	        payment['user_id'], 'credit',
107	        payment['amount'], new_balance,
108	        admin_id,
109	        description=(
110	            f'Offline payment confirmed: {payment["payment_type"]} '
111	            f'ref {payment["reference_number"]}'
112	        ),
113	        reference_id=payment_id,
114	        reference_type='offline_payment',
115	    )
116	
117	    ledger_row = conn.execute(
118	        'SELECT id FROM ledger_entries '
119	        'WHERE reference_id = ? AND reference_type = ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1',
120	        (payment_id, 'offline_payment')
121	    ).fetchone()
122	    ledger_entry_id = ledger_row['id'] if ledger_row else None
123	
124	    payment_dal.set_confirmed(conn, payment_id, admin_id, ledger_entry_id)
125	
126	    # Simulate callback in-process (no network calls)
127	    _simulate_callback(conn, payment_id, admin_id, canonical, payment['signature'])
128	
129	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'PAYMENT_CONFIRMED', user_id=admin_id,
130	                    entity_type='offline_payment', entity_id=payment_id,
131	                    details={
132	                        'amount':      payment['amount'],
133	                        'user_id':     payment['user_id'],
134	                        'new_balance': new_balance,
135	                    })
136	    return {'payment_id': payment_id, 'new_balance': new_balance}
137	
138	
139	def _simulate_callback(conn, payment_id: int, admin_id: int,
140	                       canonical: dict, stored_signature: str) -> None:
141	    """
142	    Simulate an external payment provider callback entirely in-process.
143	    Re-verifies the HMAC signature and logs the outcome as PAYMENT_CALLBACK_FIRED.
144	    No network calls are made.
145	    """
146	    verified = verify_payment_signature(canonical, stored_signature)
147	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'PAYMENT_CALLBACK_FIRED', user_id=admin_id,
148	                    entity_type='offline_payment', entity_id=payment_id,
149	                    details={
150	                        'verified':      verified,
151	                        'payment_type':  canonical['payment_type'],
152	                        'amount':        canonical['amount'],
153	                        'callback_type': 'local_simulation',
154	                    })
155	
156	
157	def refund_payment(conn, payment_id: int, admin_id: int,
158	                   reason: str = None) -> dict:
159	    """
160	    Refund a confirmed payment by inserting a reversing debit ledger entry.
161	    Returns {'payment_id': int, 'new_balance': float}.
162	    Raises LookupError or ValueError.
163	    """
164	    payment = payment_dal.get_by_id(conn, payment_id)
165	    if not payment:
166	        raise LookupError('Payment not found.')
167	    if payment['status'] != 'confirmed':
168	        raise ValueError(
169	            f'Only confirmed payments can be refunded; '
170	            f'current status: {payment["status"]}.'
171	        )
172	
173	    user = user_dal.get_by_id(conn, payment['user_id'])
174	    if not user:
175	        raise LookupError('Payer user not found.')
176	    if user['credit_balance'] < payment['amount']:
177	        raise ValueError('Insufficient balance for refund.')
178	
179	    new_balance = round(user['credit_balance'] - payment['amount'], 4)
180	    user_dal.update_fields(conn, payment['user_id'], credit_balance=new_balance)
181	
182	    desc = (
183	        f'Offline payment refund: {payment["payment_type"]} '
184	        f'ref {payment["reference_number"]}'
185	        + (f' — {reason}' if reason else '')
186	    )
187	    ledger_dal.insert_entry(
188	        conn,
189	        payment['user_id'], 'debit',
190	        payment['amount'], new_balance,
191	        admin_id,
192	        description=desc,
193	        reference_id=payment_id,
194	        reference_type='offline_payment_refund',
195	    )
196	
197	    refund_row = conn.execute(
198	        'SELECT id FROM ledger_entries '
199	        'WHERE reference_id = ? AND reference_type = ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1',
200	        (payment_id, 'offline_payment_refund')
201	    ).fetchone()
202	    refund_entry_id = refund_row['id'] if refund_row else None
203	
204	    payment_dal.set_refunded(conn, payment_id, refund_entry_id)
205	
206	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'PAYMENT_REFUNDED', user_id=admin_id,
207	                    entity_type='offline_payment', entity_id=payment_id,
208	                    details={
209	                        'amount':      payment['amount'],
210	                        'user_id':     payment['user_id'],
211	                        'new_balance': new_balance,
212	                        'reason':      reason,
213	                    })
214	    return {'payment_id': payment_id, 'new_balance': new_balance}
215

1	"""
2	Offline payment routes.
3	
4	POST /api/payments/submit          — user submits a payment claim
5	POST /api/payments/<id>/confirm    — admin confirms + fires callback simulation
6	POST /api/payments/<id>/refund     — admin refunds a confirmed payment
7	GET  /api/payments/                — list payments (admin: all; user: own)
8	GET  /api/payments/<id>            — get single payment (admin: any; user: own)
9	"""
10	
11	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
12	from app.models import db
13	from app.utils import login_required, admin_required
14	from app.services import payment_service
15	from app.dal import payment_dal
16	
17	payments_bp = Blueprint('payments', __name__)
18	
19	VALID_STATUSES = ('pending', 'confirmed', 'refunded', 'failed')
20	VALID_TYPES    = ('cash', 'check', 'ach')
21	
22	
23	@payments_bp.route('/submit', methods=['POST'])
24	@login_required
25	def submit():
26	    """
27	    Body:
28	      amount          float   required
29	      payment_type    string  required  (cash | check | ach)
30	      reference_number string required
31	      notes           string  optional
32	    """
33	    d = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
34	    try:
35	        amount = float(d.get('amount', 0))
36	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
37	        return jsonify({'error': 'amount must be a number.'}), 400
38	
39	    payment_type     = d.get('payment_type', '').lower()
40	    reference_number = d.get('reference_number', '')
41	    notes            = d.get('notes')
42	
43	    try:
44	        with db() as conn:
45	            result = payment_service.submit_payment(
46	                conn, g.user_id, amount, payment_type, reference_number, notes
47	            )
48	    except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
49	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
50	
51	    return jsonify({
52	        'message':    'Payment submitted and awaiting confirmation.',
53	        'payment_id': result['payment_id'],
54	        'signature':  result['signature'],
55	    }), 201
56	
57	
58	@payments_bp.route('/<int:payment_id>/confirm', methods=['POST'])
59	@admin_required
60	def confirm(payment_id):
61	    """Confirm a pending payment and credit the user's account."""
62	    try:
63	        with db() as conn:
64	            result = payment_service.confirm_payment(conn, payment_id, g.user_id)
65	    except LookupError as e:
66	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 404
67	    except ValueError as e:
68	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
69	
70	    return jsonify({
71	        'message':     'Payment confirmed and account credited.',
72	        'payment_id':  result['payment_id'],
73	        'new_balance': result['new_balance'],
74	    }), 200
75	
76	
77	@payments_bp.route('/<int:payment_id>/refund', methods=['POST'])
78	@admin_required
79	def refund(payment_id):
80	    """
81	    Refund a confirmed payment via a reversing ledger debit.
82	    Body (optional): { "reason": "..." }
83	    """
84	    d      = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
85	    reason = d.get('reason')
86	
87	    try:
88	        with db() as conn:
89	            result = payment_service.refund_payment(conn, payment_id, g.user_id, reason)
90	    except LookupError as e:
91	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 404
92	    except ValueError as e:
93	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
94	
95	    return jsonify({
96	        'message':     'Payment refunded.',
97	        'payment_id':  result['payment_id'],
98	        'new_balance': result['new_balance'],
99	    }), 200
100	
101	
102	@payments_bp.route('/', methods=['GET'])
103	@login_required
104	def list_payments():
105	    """
106	    Query params:
107	      status        — filter by status (pending | confirmed | refunded | failed)
108	      payment_type  — filter by type (cash | check | ach)
109	      page, per_page
110	    Admins see all payments; regular users see only their own.
111	    """
112	    page     = max(1, int(request.args.get('page', 1)))
113	    per_page = min(100, int(request.args.get('per_page', 20)))
114	
115	    status       = request.args.get('status')
116	    payment_type = request.args.get('payment_type')
117	
118	    if status and status not in VALID_STATUSES:
119	        return jsonify({'error': f'status must be one of: {", ".join(VALID_STATUSES)}.'}), 400
120	    if payment_type and payment_type not in VALID_TYPES:
121	        return jsonify({'error': f'payment_type must be one of: {", ".join(VALID_TYPES)}.'}), 400
122	
123	    # Non-admin users can only see their own payments
124	    filter_user_id = None if g.role == 'admin' else g.user_id
125	
126	    with db() as conn:
127	        rows, total = payment_dal.list_payments(
128	            conn,
129	            user_id=filter_user_id,
130	            status=status,
131	            payment_type=payment_type,
132	            limit=per_page,
133	            offset=(page - 1) * per_page,
134	        )
135	
136	    return jsonify({'payments': rows, 'total': total, 'page': page}), 200
137	
138	
139	@payments_bp.route('/<int:payment_id>', methods=['GET'])
140	@login_required
141	def get_payment(payment_id):
142	    """Admins can fetch any payment; users can only fetch their own."""
143	    with db() as conn:
144	        payment = payment_dal.get_by_id(conn, payment_id)
145	
146	    if not payment:
147	        return jsonify({'error': 'Payment not found.'}), 404
148	    if g.role != 'admin' and payment['user_id'] != g.user_id:
149	        return jsonify({'error': 'Access denied.'}), 403
150	
151	    return jsonify({'payment': payment}), 200
152

1	"""
2	Offline Payment DAL.
3	
4	All writes go through this module.
5	Statuses:  pending → confirmed → refunded
6	           pending → failed
7	"""
8	
9	from app.models import row_to_dict, rows_to_list
10	from app.utils import utcnow
11	
12	
13	def create(conn, user_id: int, amount: float, payment_type: str,
14	           reference_number: str, signature: str,
15	           notes: str = None) -> int:
16	    """Insert a new pending payment and return its id."""
17	    now = utcnow()
18	    cur = conn.execute(
19	        'INSERT INTO offline_payments '
20	        '(user_id, amount, payment_type, reference_number, signature, '
21	        ' notes, created_at, updated_at) '
22	        'VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)',
23	        (user_id, amount, payment_type, reference_number, signature,
24	         notes, now, now)
25	    )
26	    return cur.lastrowid
27	
28	
29	def get_by_id(conn, payment_id: int) -> dict | None:
30	    return row_to_dict(conn.execute(
31	        'SELECT * FROM offline_payments WHERE id = ?', (payment_id,)
32	    ).fetchone())
33	
34	
35	def list_payments(conn, user_id: int = None, status: str = None,
36	                  payment_type: str = None,
37	                  limit: int = 50, offset: int = 0) -> tuple[list, int]:
38	    query = 'SELECT * FROM offline_payments WHERE 1=1'
39	    params = []
40	    if user_id:
41	        query += ' AND user_id = ?'
42	        params.append(int(user_id))
43	    if status:
44	        query += ' AND status = ?'
45	        params.append(status)
46	    if payment_type:
47	        query += ' AND payment_type = ?'
48	        params.append(payment_type)
49	
50	    total = conn.execute(f'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({query})', params).fetchone()[0]
51	    query += ' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?'
52	    rows = rows_to_list(conn.execute(query, params + [limit, offset]).fetchall())
53	    return rows, total
54	
55	
56	def set_confirmed(conn, payment_id: int, admin_id: int,
57	                  ledger_entry_id: int) -> None:
58	    conn.execute(
59	        'UPDATE offline_payments '
60	        'SET status = ?, confirmed_by = ?, confirmed_at = ?, '
61	        '    ledger_entry_id = ?, updated_at = ? '
62	        'WHERE id = ?',
63	        ('confirmed', admin_id, utcnow(), ledger_entry_id, utcnow(), payment_id)
64	    )
65	
66	
67	def set_failed(conn, payment_id: int) -> None:
68	    conn.execute(
69	        'UPDATE offline_payments SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?',
70	        ('failed', utcnow(), payment_id)
71	    )
72	
73	
74	def set_refunded(conn, payment_id: int, refund_entry_id: int) -> None:
75	    conn.execute(
76	        'UPDATE offline_payments '
77	        'SET status = ?, refund_entry_id = ?, updated_at = ? '
78	        'WHERE id = ?',
79	        ('refunded', refund_entry_id, utcnow(), payment_id)
80	    )
81

1	"""
2	Database schema initialization and connection management.
3	All timestamps are stored as ISO-8601 TEXT (UTC).
4	Ledger and audit_log are tamper-evident via chained SHA-256 hashes.
5	No UPDATE/DELETE is permitted on ledger_entries or audit_logs (insert-only).
6	"""
7	
8	import sqlite3
9	from contextlib import contextmanager
10	from config import Config
11	
12	SCHEMA = """
13	PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;
14	PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;
15	
16	-- =========================================================
17	-- 1. USERS & AUTH
18	-- =========================================================
19	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
20	    id                    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
21	    username              TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL,
22	    email                 TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL,
23	    password_hash         TEXT    NOT NULL,
24	    role                  TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user'
25	                              CHECK(role IN ('user', 'admin', 'auditor')),
26	    is_active             INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
27	                              CHECK(is_active IN (0, 1)),
28	    credit_balance        REAL    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0
29	                              CHECK(credit_balance >= 0),
30	    failed_login_attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
31	    lockout_until         TEXT,                       -- ISO-8601 or NULL
32	    muted_until           TEXT,                       -- ISO-8601 or NULL (temp mute)
33	    created_at            TEXT    NOT NULL,
34	    updated_at            TEXT    NOT NULL
35	);
36	
37	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_username  ON users(username);
38	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_email     ON users(email);
39	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_role      ON users(role);
40	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_is_active ON users(is_active);
41	
42	-- =========================================================
43	-- 2. IDENTITY VERIFICATION
44	-- Sensitive document content is AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest.
45	-- =========================================================
46	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS identity_verifications (
47	    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    user_id             INTEGER NOT NULL,
49	    document_type       TEXT    NOT NULL
50	                            CHECK(document_type IN (
51	                                'passport','national_id','drivers_license','utility_bill'
52	                            )),
53	    document_data_enc   TEXT    NOT NULL,             -- AES-256-GCM ciphertext (base64)
54	    document_fingerprint TEXT,                        -- SHA-256 hex of raw bytes (before enc)
55	    content_type        TEXT,                         -- validated MIME type
56	    file_size_bytes     INTEGER,                      -- raw file size
57	    status              TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
58	                            CHECK(status IN ('pending','verified','rejected')),
59	    submitted_at        TEXT    NOT NULL,
60	    reviewed_at         TEXT,
61	    reviewer_id         INTEGER,
62	    notes               TEXT,
63	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id)     REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
64	    FOREIGN KEY (reviewer_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT
65	);
66	
67	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_iv_user_id ON identity_verifications(user_id);
68	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_iv_status  ON identity_verifications(status);
69	
70	-- =========================================================
71	-- 3a. MATCHING PROFILES
72	-- =========================================================
73	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS matching_profiles (
74	    id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
75	    user_id        INTEGER UNIQUE NOT NULL,

373	-- =========================================================
374	-- 12. OFFLINE PAYMENTS
375	-- Cash / check / ACH reference payments submitted by users and
376	-- confirmed by admins.  Each record carries an HMAC-SHA256
377	-- signature over the canonical payload so any tampering is
378	-- detectable without an external service.
379	-- Ledger integration: confirm → credit; refund → debit (reference_type='offline_payment_refund').
380	-- =========================================================
381	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS offline_payments (
382	    id               INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
383	    user_id          INTEGER NOT NULL,
384	    amount           REAL    NOT NULL CHECK(amount > 0),
385	    payment_type     TEXT    NOT NULL
386	                         CHECK(payment_type IN ('cash','check','ach')),
387	    reference_number TEXT    NOT NULL,
388	    status           TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
389	                         CHECK(status IN ('pending','confirmed','refunded','failed')),
390	    signature        TEXT    NOT NULL,            -- HMAC-SHA256 hex of canonical payload
391	    ledger_entry_id  INTEGER,                     -- set after ledger credit is posted
392	    refund_entry_id  INTEGER,                     -- set when refunded
393	    confirmed_by     INTEGER,
394	    confirmed_at     TEXT,
395	    notes            TEXT,
396	    created_at       TEXT    NOT NULL,
397	    updated_at       TEXT    NOT NULL,
398	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id)         REFERENCES users(id)         ON DELETE RESTRICT,
399	    FOREIGN KEY (confirmed_by)    REFERENCES users(id)         ON DELETE RESTRICT,
400	    FOREIGN KEY (ledger_entry_id) REFERENCES ledger_entries(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
401	    FOREIGN KEY (refund_entry_id) REFERENCES ledger_entries(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT
402	);
403	
404	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_op_user_id    ON offline_payments(user_id);
405	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_op_status     ON offline_payments(status);
406	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_op_created_at ON offline_payments(created_at);
407	
408	-- =========================================================
409	-- 10. IDEMPOTENCY KEYS
410	-- Caches responses for critical mutating operations.
411	-- =========================================================
412	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS idempotency_keys (
413	    key_value       TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
414	    response_status INTEGER NOT NULL,
415	    response_body   TEXT    NOT NULL,
416	    created_at      TEXT    NOT NULL,
417	    user_id         INTEGER,
418	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
419	);
420	
421	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_idem_user_id ON idempotency_keys(user_id);
422	"""
423	
424	
425	def get_connection() -> sqlite3.Connection:
426	    conn = sqlite3.connect(Config.DATABASE_PATH)
427	    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
428	    conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
429	    conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
430	    return conn
431	
432	
433	@contextmanager
434	def db():
435	    """Context manager yielding a database connection with auto-commit/rollback."""
436	    conn = get_connection()
437	    try:
438	        yield conn
439	        conn.commit()
440	    except Exception:
441	        conn.rollback()
442	        raise
443	    finally:
444	        conn.close()
445	
446	
447	def init_db():
448	    """Initialize schema and run any incremental migrations."""
449	    conn = get_connection()
450	    conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
451	    # Incremental migrations for existing databases
452	    _migrate(conn)
453	    conn.commit()
454	    conn.close()
455	
456	
457	def _migrate(conn):
458	    """Apply additive column migrations that cannot be in CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS."""
459	    migrations = [
460	        ("users", "muted_until",   "ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN muted_until TEXT"),
461	        ("sessions", "cancelled_at",  "ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN cancelled_at TEXT"),
462	        ("sessions", "cancel_reason", "ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN cancel_reason TEXT"),
463	        ("identity_verifications", "document_fingerprint",
464	         "ALTER TABLE identity_verifications ADD COLUMN document_fingerprint TEXT"),
465	        ("identity_verifications", "content_type",
466	         "ALTER TABLE identity_verifications ADD COLUMN content_type TEXT"),
467	        ("identity_verifications", "file_size_bytes",
468	         "ALTER TABLE identity_verifications ADD COLUMN file_size_bytes INTEGER"),
469	    ]
470	    existing_cols = {}
471	    for table, col, sql in migrations:
472	        if table not in existing_cols:
473	            existing_cols[table] = {
474	                row[1] for row in conn.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})").fetchall()
475	            }
476	        if col not in existing_cols[table]:
477	            conn.execute(sql)
478	            existing_cols[table].add(col)
479	
480	
481	def row_to_dict(row) -> dict:
482	    if row is None:
483	        return None
484	    return dict(row)
485	
486	
487	def rows_to_list(rows) -> list:
488	    return [dict(r) for r in rows]
489

1	"""
2	Ledger DAL — INSERT ONLY.
3	Never issues UPDATE or DELETE on ledger_entries.
4	"""
5	
6	from app.models import row_to_dict, rows_to_list
7	from app.utils import utcnow, hash_ledger_entry
8	
9	
10	def get_last_hash(conn) -> str | None:
11	    row = conn.execute(
12	        'SELECT entry_hash FROM ledger_entries ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1'
13	    ).fetchone()
14	    return row['entry_hash'] if row else None
15	
16	
17	def insert_entry(conn, user_id: int, transaction_type: str,
18	                 amount: float, balance_after: float,
19	                 created_by: int, description: str = None,
20	                 reference_id: int = None, reference_type: str = None,
21	                 idempotency_key: str = None) -> str:
22	    """
23	    Insert a ledger entry and return the new entry_hash.
24	    Raises on any constraint violation (duplicate idempotency_key, etc.).
25	    """
26	    now = utcnow()
27	    previous_hash = get_last_hash(conn)
28	    entry_data = {
29	        'user_id': user_id,
30	        'transaction_type': transaction_type,
31	        'amount': amount,
32	        'balance_after': balance_after,
33	        'created_at': now,
34	        'created_by': created_by,
35	        'description': description or '',
36	    }
37	    entry_hash = hash_ledger_entry(entry_data, previous_hash)
38	
39	    conn.execute(
40	        'INSERT INTO ledger_entries '
41	        '(entry_hash, previous_hash, user_id, transaction_type, amount, '
42	        'balance_after, reference_id, reference_type, description, '
43	        'created_at, created_by, idempotency_key) '
44	        'VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)',
45	        (entry_hash, previous_hash, user_id, transaction_type, amount,
46	         balance_after, reference_id, reference_type, description,
47	         now, created_by, idempotency_key)
48	    )
49	    return entry_hash
50	
51	
52	def list_entries(conn, user_id: int = None,
53	                 limit: int = 20, offset: int = 0,
54	                 privileged: bool = False) -> tuple[list, int]:
55	    if privileged:
56	        query = (
57	            'SELECT l.id, l.user_id, u.username, l.transaction_type, '
58	            'l.amount, l.balance_after, l.description, l.created_at, '
59	            'l.entry_hash, l.reference_type, l.reference_id '
60	            'FROM ledger_entries l JOIN users u ON l.user_id = u.id '
61	            'WHERE 1=1'
62	        )
63	        params = []
64	        if user_id:
65	            query += ' AND l.user_id = ?'
66	            params.append(user_id)
67	    else:
68	        query = (
69	            'SELECT id, transaction_type, amount, balance_after, '
70	            'description, created_at, reference_type, reference_id '
71	            'FROM ledger_entries WHERE user_id = ?'
72	        )
73	        params = [user_id]
74	
75	    total = conn.execute(f'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({query})', params).fetchone()[0]
76	    query += ' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?'
77	    rows = rows_to_list(conn.execute(query, params + [limit, offset]).fetchall())
78	    return rows, total
79	
80	
81	def get_all_ordered(conn) -> list:
82	    """Return all entries ordered by id ASC (for chain verification)."""
83	    return rows_to_list(conn.execute(
84	        'SELECT * FROM ledger_entries ORDER BY id ASC'
85	    ).fetchall())

97	    for magic, mime in _MAGIC_SIGNATURES:

201	def login_required(f):
202	    @wraps(f)
203	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
204	        token = _get_token_from_request()
205	        if not token:
206	            return jsonify({'error': 'Authentication required.'}), 401
207	        claims = decode_token(token)
208	        if not claims:
209	            return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid or expired token.'}), 401
210	        g.user_id = claims['sub']
211	        g.username = claims['username']
212	        g.role = claims['role']
213	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
214	    return decorated

223	                return jsonify({'error': 'Insufficient permissions.'}), 403
224	            return f(*args, **kwargs)

240	def check_idempotency(conn, key: str, user_id: int):
241	    """
242	    Returns (already_exists: bool, cached_response: dict | None).
243	    If already_exists, caller should return the cached response immediately.
244	    """
245	    if not key:
246	        return False, None
247	    row = conn.execute(
248	        'SELECT response_status, response_body FROM idempotency_keys '
249	        'WHERE key_value = ? AND user_id = ?',
250	        (key, user_id)
251	    ).fetchone()
252	    if row:
253	        return True, {'status': row['response_status'],
254	                      'body': json.loads(row['response_body'])}
255	    return False, None

The browser (or proxy) sent a request that this server could not understand.

---

## README.md

The README describes a **SkillShare Platform** — a peer skill-exchange SPA with:
- JWT auth, ledger/credit system, identity verification, admin moderation, analytics, offline payments
- Default admin: `admin` / `Admin@123456!`
- Single Docker Compose command to run: `docker compose up` (port 8000)
- All REST endpoints under `/api/`; SPA served at `/`
- Tests run via `bash run_tests.sh`

Key API endpoints documented:
- Auth: register, login, me, change-password
- Ledger: balance, credit (admin), transfer, invoices
- Payments: submit, confirm (admin), refund (admin)
- Audit: logs, summary, verify (hash chain)
- Analytics: KPIs, CSV export
- Admin: violations, ban/unban users
- (Matching endpoints are NOT listed in the README's summary table — a notable gap)

Security stack: PBKDF2-SHA256 (600k iterations), HS256 JWT (24h), AES-256-GCM, HMAC-SHA256 for payments, SHA-256 hash chains for audit and ledger.

---

## templates/index.html

Minimal SPA shell — just a `<div id="app">` placeholder and two asset tags:
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/css/style.css">
<script src="/static/js/app.js"></script>
```
Everything is rendered client-side by the JS modules. The HTML itself is 15 lines total.

---

## app/routes/matching.py (Full Content)

Located at `/c/Users/user/Desktop/eaglepoint_new/Week-2/TASK-57/repo/app/routes/matching.py`

A Flask Blueprint (`matching_bp`) with these routes, all under `@login_required`:

| Method | Route | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/profile` | Get own matching profile |
| POST/PUT | `/profile` | Upsert profile (skills_offered, skills_needed, availability, bio, is_active) |
| GET | `/search` | Search peers by skill with pagination |
| POST | `/sessions` | Request a session (supports Idempotency-Key) |
| GET | `/sessions` | List sessions for current user |
| GET | `/sessions/<id>` | Get single session (participants/admin/auditor only) |
| PUT | `/sessions/<id>` | Update session status |
| GET | `/queue/<id>` | Get a single queue entry |
| PUT | `/queue/<id>/cancel` | Cancel a waiting queue entry |
| POST | `/queue` | Join the auto-match queue |
| POST | `/queue/match` | Trigger auto-match attempt |
| GET | `/queue` | List queue (own entries for users, all for admin) |
| POST | `/block` | Block a user |
| DELETE | `/block/<id>` | Unblock a user |
| GET | `/block` | List blocked users |

---

## app/services/matching_service.py (Full Content)

Located at `/c/Users/user/Desktop/eaglepoint_new/Week-2/TASK-57/repo/app/services/matching_service.py`

178 lines. Business logic layer with these functions:

**`save_profile(conn, user_id, skills_offered, skills_needed, availability, bio, is_active)`**
- Enforces `guard_is_active`
- Trims skills to 64 chars, max 20 per list; bio to 500 chars
- Calls `matching_dal.upsert_profile`, writes audit event

**`search_peers(conn, requester_id, skill)`**
- Excludes blocked users
- Filters by skill substring match (case-insensitive) if skill provided
- Returns sanitized profile dicts (no availability field exposed)

**`request_session(conn, initiator_id, participant_id, description, duration_minutes, credit_amount, scheduled_at, idempotency_key)`**
- Enforces `guard_can_act` (ban/mute/credit/dispute checks)
- Prevents self-sessions, validates participant exists and is active
- Checks block relationship
- Calls `session_dal.create`, writes audit event

**`update_session_status(conn, session_id, actor_id, new_status, actor_role, cancel_reason)`**
- Valid transitions: `pending → [active, cancelled]`, `active → [completed, cancelled]`
- Only participants or admins may update
- Writes audit event

**`join_queue(conn, user_id, skill, priority, expires_at)`**
- Enforces `guard_can_act`
- Requires non-empty skill
- Calls `matching_dal.enqueue`, writes audit event

**`auto_match(conn, user_id, skill)`**
- Enforces `guard_can_act`, excludes blocked users
- Calls `matching_dal.find_waiting_match`, creates session, updates queue entry, writes audit

**`block_user(conn, blocker_id, blocked_id, reason)`** / **`unblock_user(...)`**
- Prevents self-blocking; enforces `guard_is_active`

---

## static/ Directory (All Files)

```
static/css/style.css
static/js/admin.js
static/js/api.js
static/js/app.js
static/js/auth.js
static/js/dashboard.js
static/js/ledger.js
static/js/matching.js
static/js/utils.js
static/js/verification.js
```

9 JS modules total. The frontend is a vanilla ES-module SPA (no build tool, no bundler, no framework — just native browser modules).

---

## Frontend Test Files

**None exist.** There are no `.test.js`, `.spec.js`, Jest configs, Cypress configs, Playwright configs, or any other frontend test infrastructure anywhere in the repo. The frontend is completely untested.

---

## run_tests.sh

A simple 68-line bash script:
1. Checks for `pytest` (installs `requirements.txt` if missing)
2. Runs `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short -q`
3. Runs `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -q`
4. Reports combined pass/fail and exits with appropriate code

Uses `set -euo pipefail` but saves exit codes so both suites always run even if one fails.

---

## Test Suite Inventory

**unit_tests/** (6 test files, no matching tests):
- `test_audit.py` — audit hash chain
- `test_auth.py` — auth service
- `test_ledger.py` — ledger service
- `test_payment.py` — payment service
- `test_rating.py` — rating service
- `test_utils.py` — utility functions

**API_tests/** (7 test files, no matching API tests):
- `test_admin_api.py`
- `test_analytics_api.py`
- `test_audit_api.py`
- `test_auth_api.py`
- `test_ledger_api.py`
- `test_payments_api.py`
- `test_users_api.py`

**Notable gap:** There is no `test_matching*.py` in either `unit_tests/` or `API_tests/`, and no frontend tests at all. The entire matching subsystem (profiles, search, sessions, queue, blacklist) has zero automated test coverage.

13 14 15 16 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. 1	"""
2	Matching service — peer search, session lifecycle, queue, blacklist.
3	Guards (ban/mute/credit/disputes) are enforced before every mutating action.
4	"""
5	
6	import json
7	from app.services.guards import guard_can_act, guard_is_active
8	from app.dal import matching_dal, session_dal, audit_dal, user_dal
9	
10	VALID_SESSION_TRANSITIONS = {
11	    'pending': ['active', 'cancelled'],
12	    'active':  ['completed', 'cancelled'],
13	}
14	
15	
16	# ---- Profile ------------------------------------------------------------
17	
18	def save_profile(conn, user_id: int, skills_offered: list,
19	                 skills_needed: list, availability: dict,
20	                 bio: str, is_active: bool) -> None:
21	    ok, reason = guard_is_active(conn, user_id)
22	    if not ok:
23	        raise PermissionError(reason)
24	
25	    skills_offered = [str(s).strip()[:64] for s in skills_offered if str(s).strip()][:20]
26	    skills_needed  = [str(s).strip()[:64] for s in skills_needed  if str(s).strip()][:20]
27	
28	    existing = matching_dal.get_profile(conn, user_id)
29	    matching_dal.upsert_profile(conn, user_id, skills_offered, skills_needed,
30	                                 availability, bio[:500], is_active)
31	    action = 'MATCHING_PROFILE_UPDATED' if existing else 'MATCHING_PROFILE_CREATED'
32	    audit_dal.write(conn, action, user_id=user_id,
33	                    entity_type='matching_profile', entity_id=user_id)
34	
35	
36	def search_peers(conn, requester_id: int, skill: str = '') -> list:
37	    blocked_ids = matching_dal.get_blocked_ids(conn, requester_id)
38	    profiles = matching_dal.search_profiles(conn, requester_id, blocked_ids)
39	    if skill:
40	        skill_lower = skill.lower()
41	        profiles = [
42	            p for p in profiles
43	            if any(skill_lower in s.lower()
44	                   for s in json.loads(p.get('skills_offered', '[]')))
45	        ]
46	    result = []
47	    for p in profiles:
48	        result.append({
49	            'user_id':       p['user_id'],
50	            'username':      p['username'],
51	            'skills_offered': json.loads(p['skills_offered']),
52	            'skills_needed':  json.loads(p['skills_needed']),
53	            'bio':            p['bio'],
54	        })
55	    return result
56	
57	
58	# ---- Sessions -----------------------------------------------------------
59	
60	def request_session(conn, initiator_id: int, participant_id: int,
61	                    description: str, duration_minutes: int,
62	                    credit_amount: float, scheduled_at: str,
63	                    idempotency_key: str = None) -> int:
64	    # Guard: initiator must pass all eligibility checks
65	    ok, reason = guard_can_act(conn, initiator_id)
66	    if not ok:
67	        raise PermissionError(reason)
68	
69	    if participant_id == initiator_id:
70	        raise ValueError('Cannot create a session with yourself.')
71	
72	    participant = user_dal.get_by_id(conn, participant_id)
73	    if not participant or not participant['is_active']:
74	        raise LookupError('Participant not found or inactive.')
75	
76	    if matching_dal.is_blocked(conn, initiator_id, participant_id):
77	        raise PermissionError('Cannot create a session with a blocked user.')
78	
79	    session_id = session_dal.create(
80	        conn, initiator_id, participant_id,
81	        description, duration_minutes, credit_amount,
82	        scheduled_at, idempotency_key
83	    )
84	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'SESSION_REQUESTED', user_id=initiator_id,
85	                    entity_type='session', entity_id=session_id)
86	    return session_id
87	
88	
89	def update_session_status(conn, session_id: int, actor_id: int,
90	                          new_status: str, actor_role: str = 'user',
91	                          cancel_reason: str = None) -> None:
92	    session = session_dal.get_by_id(conn, session_id)
93	    if not session:
94	        raise LookupError('Session not found.')
95	
96	    # Only participants or admins may update
97	    if actor_id not in (session['initiator_id'], session['participant_id']) \
98	            and actor_role != 'admin':
99	        raise PermissionError('Access denied.')
100	
101	    allowed = VALID_SESSION_TRANSITIONS.get(session['status'], [])
102	    if new_status not in allowed:
103	        raise ValueError(
104	            f'Cannot transition from "{session["status"]}" to "{new_status}".'
105	        )
106	
107	    session_dal.update_status(conn, session_id, new_status, cancel_reason)
108	    audit_dal.write(conn, f'SESSION_{new_status.upper()}',
109	                    user_id=actor_id, entity_type='session', entity_id=session_id,
110	                    details={'old_status': session['status']})
111	
112	
113	# ---- Queue --------------------------------------------------------------
114	
115	def join_queue(conn, user_id: int, skill: str,
116	               priority: int = 0, expires_at: str = None) -> int:
117	    ok, reason = guard_can_act(conn, user_id)
118	    if not ok:
119	        raise PermissionError(reason)
120	    if not skill or not skill.strip():
121	        raise ValueError('skill is required.')
122	
123	    entry_id = matching_dal.enqueue(conn, user_id, skill, priority, expires_at)
124	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'QUEUE_JOINED', user_id=user_id,
125	                    entity_type='matching_queue', entity_id=entry_id,
126	                    details={'skill': skill})
127	    return entry_id
128	
129	
130	def auto_match(conn, user_id: int, skill: str) -> dict | None:
131	    """
132	    Attempt to auto-match user_id against the queue for `skill`.
133	    Returns the matched queue entry dict, or None if no match found.
134	    """
135	    ok, reason = guard_can_act(conn, user_id)
136	    if not ok:
137	        raise PermissionError(reason)
138	
139	    blocked_ids = matching_dal.get_blocked_ids(conn, user_id)
140	    match = matching_dal.find_waiting_match(conn, skill, user_id, blocked_ids)
141	    if not match:
142	        return None
143	
144	    # Create the session
145	    session_id = session_dal.create(
146	        conn, user_id, match['user_id'],
147	        f'Auto-matched on skill: {skill}', None, 0.0, None
148	    )
149	    # Update both queue entries
150	    matching_dal.update_queue_entry(
151	        conn, match['id'],
152	        status='matched', matched_to=user_id, session_id=session_id
153	    )
154	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'AUTO_MATCHED', user_id=user_id,
155	                    entity_type='session', entity_id=session_id,
156	                    details={'matched_with': match['user_id'], 'skill': skill})
157	    return {**match, 'session_id': session_id}
158	
159	
160	# ---- Blacklist ----------------------------------------------------------
161	
162	def block_user(conn, blocker_id: int, blocked_id: int,
163	               reason: str = None) -> None:
164	    ok, r = guard_is_active(conn, blocker_id)
165	    if not ok:
166	        raise PermissionError(r)
167	    if blocker_id == blocked_id:
168	        raise ValueError('Cannot block yourself.')
169	    matching_dal.add_block(conn, blocker_id, blocked_id, reason)
170	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'USER_BLOCKED', user_id=blocker_id,
171	                    entity_type='user', entity_id=blocked_id)
172	
173	
174	def unblock_user(conn, blocker_id: int, blocked_id: int) -> None:
175	    matching_dal.remove_block(conn, blocker_id, blocked_id)
176	    audit_dal.write(conn, 'USER_UNBLOCKED', user_id=blocker_id,
177	                    entity_type='user', entity_id=blocked_id)
178

1	"""Matching profiles, queue, and blacklist DAL."""
2	
3	import json
4	from app.models import row_to_dict, rows_to_list
5	from app.utils import utcnow
6	
7	
8	# ---- Profiles -----------------------------------------------------------
9	
10	def get_profile(conn, user_id: int) -> dict | None:
11	    return row_to_dict(conn.execute(
12	        'SELECT * FROM matching_profiles WHERE user_id = ?', (user_id,)
13	    ).fetchone())
14	
15	
16	def upsert_profile(conn, user_id: int, skills_offered: list,
17	                   skills_needed: list, availability: dict,
18	                   bio: str, is_active: bool) -> None:
19	    now = utcnow()
20	    existing = conn.execute(
21	        'SELECT id FROM matching_profiles WHERE user_id = ?', (user_id,)
22	    ).fetchone()
23	    if existing:
24	        conn.execute(
25	            'UPDATE matching_profiles SET skills_offered=?, skills_needed=?, '
26	            'availability=?, bio=?, is_active=?, updated_at=? WHERE user_id=?',
27	            (json.dumps(skills_offered), json.dumps(skills_needed),
28	             json.dumps(availability), bio, 1 if is_active else 0, now, user_id)
29	        )
30	    else:
31	        conn.execute(
32	            'INSERT INTO matching_profiles '
33	            '(user_id, skills_offered, skills_needed, availability, bio, '
34	            'is_active, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)',
35	            (user_id, json.dumps(skills_offered), json.dumps(skills_needed),
36	             json.dumps(availability), bio, 1 if is_active else 0, now, now)
37	        )
38	
39	
40	def search_profiles(conn, exclude_user_id: int,
41	                    blocked_ids: set) -> list:
42	    """Return all active profiles excluding the requester and blocked users."""
43	    rows = rows_to_list(conn.execute(
44	        'SELECT mp.*, u.username '
45	        'FROM matching_profiles mp JOIN users u ON mp.user_id = u.id '
46	        'WHERE mp.is_active = 1 AND mp.user_id != ? AND u.is_active = 1',
47	        (exclude_user_id,)
48	    ).fetchall())
49	    return [r for r in rows if r['user_id'] not in blocked_ids]
50	
51	
52	# ---- Queue --------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	def enqueue(conn, user_id: int, skill: str,
55	            priority: int = 0, expires_at: str = None) -> int:
56	    now = utcnow()
57	    cur = conn.execute(
58	        'INSERT INTO matching_queue '
59	        '(user_id, skill, priority, status, expires_at, created_at, updated_at) '
60	        'VALUES (?, ?, ?, "waiting", ?, ?, ?)',
61	        (user_id, skill.lower().strip(), priority, expires_at, now, now)
62	    )
63	    return cur.lastrowid
64	
65	
66	def get_queue_entry(conn, entry_id: int) -> dict | None:
67	    return row_to_dict(conn.execute(
68	        'SELECT * FROM matching_queue WHERE id = ?', (entry_id,)
69	    ).fetchone())
70	
71	
72	def find_waiting_match(conn, skill: str, exclude_user_id: int,
73	                       blocked_ids: set) -> dict | None:
74	    """Find the highest-priority waiting entry for a skill."""
75	    rows = rows_to_list(conn.execute(
76	        'SELECT * FROM matching_queue '
77	        'WHERE skill = ? AND status = "waiting" AND user_id != ? '
78	        'ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC',
79	        (skill.lower().strip(), exclude_user_id)
80	    ).fetchall())
81	    for row in rows:
82	        if row['user_id'] not in blocked_ids:
83	            return row
84	    return None
85	
86	
87	def update_queue_entry(conn, entry_id: int, **fields) -> None:
88	    fields['updated_at'] = utcnow()
89	    set_clause = ', '.join(f'{k} = ?' for k in fields)
90	    conn.execute(
91	        f'UPDATE matching_queue SET {set_clause} WHERE id = ?',
92	        list(fields.values()) + [entry_id]
93	    )
94	
95	
96	def list_queue(conn, user_id: int = None, status: str = None,
97	               limit: int = 20, offset: int = 0) -> tuple[list, int]:
98	    query = ('SELECT mq.*, u.username FROM matching_queue mq '
99	             'JOIN users u ON mq.user_id = u.id WHERE 1=1')
100	    params = []
101	    if user_id:
102	        query += ' AND mq.user_id = ?'
103	        params.append(user_id)
104	    if status:
105	        query += ' AND mq.status = ?'
106	        params.append(status)
107	    total = conn.execute(f'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({query})', params).fetchone()[0]
108	    query += ' ORDER BY mq.priority DESC, mq.created_at ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?'
109	    rows = rows_to_list(conn.execute(query, params + [limit, offset]).fetchall())
110	    return rows, total
111	
112	
113	# ---- Blacklist ----------------------------------------------------------
114	
115	def add_block(conn, blocker_id: int, blocked_id: int, reason: str = None) -> None:
116	    conn.execute(
117	        'INSERT OR IGNORE INTO blacklist (blocker_id, blocked_id, reason, created_at) '
118	        'VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)',
119	        (blocker_id, blocked_id, reason, utcnow())
120	    )
121	
122	
123	def remove_block(conn, blocker_id: int, blocked_id: int) -> None:
124	    conn.execute(
125	        'DELETE FROM blacklist WHERE blocker_id = ? AND blocked_id = ?',
126	        (blocker_id, blocked_id)
127	    )
128	
129	
130	def get_blocked_ids(conn, user_id: int) -> set:
131	    """IDs that user_id has blocked OR that have blocked user_id."""
132	    rows = conn.execute(
133	        'SELECT blocked_id FROM blacklist WHERE blocker_id = ? '
134	        'UNION SELECT blocker_id FROM blacklist WHERE blocked_id = ?',
135	        (user_id, user_id)
136	    ).fetchall()
137	    return {r[0] for r in rows}
138	
139	
140	def is_blocked(conn, user_a: int, user_b: int) -> bool:
141	    row = conn.execute(
142	        'SELECT 1 FROM blacklist '
143	        'WHERE (blocker_id = ? AND blocked_id = ?) '
144	        '   OR (blocker_id = ? AND blocked_id = ?)',
145	        (user_a, user_b, user_b, user_a)
146	    ).fetchone()
147	    return row is not None
148	
149	
150	def list_blocks(conn, blocker_id: int) -> list:
151	    return rows_to_list(conn.execute(
152	        'SELECT bl.*, u.username as blocked_username '
153	        'FROM blacklist bl JOIN users u ON bl.blocked_id = u.id '
154	        'WHERE bl.blocker_id = ? ORDER BY bl.id DESC',
155	        (blocker_id,)
156	    ).fetchall())
157
